title: ginkgo: uninitialized LockTmpDir is scary
body:
Every so often my ginkgo tests fail to start, because I share `TMPDIR=/var/tmp/ginkgo` between root & rootless, and permission denied blah. I can live with that.

What really terrifies me is this failure mode:
```
[FAILED] Unexpected error:
      <*fs.PathError | 0xc0004066f0>: 
      open /timings-1: permission denied
      {
          Op: "open",
          Path: "/timings-1",
          Err: <syscall.Errno>0xd,
      }
```
To understand why that is scary, this is the failure line: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e0846ac383da353e699d8bf61b1da25d503a02cb/test/e2e/common_test.go#L199

Okay, `LockTmpDir` is obviously undefined, no big deal, except for: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e0846ac383da353e699d8bf61b1da25d503a02cb/test/e2e/common_test.go#L255-L256

Could someone please add sanity checks to ensure early bailout if `LockTmpDir` can't be set?

comments.body:
I don't understand how LockTmpDir can be ever undefined, that does not make any sense to me.

Also `os.RemoveAll()` is a NOP if the path is empty so it is already guarded. Of course `RemoveAll("/")` or `RemoveAll($HOME)` would still be trouble but that should not happen by accident.
